- Animal cells have several components: nucleus, cell membrane, cytoplasm, mitochondria, etc.
- The nucleus contains genetic material and regulates cellular activity.
- Cell membranes allow substances to enter and exit cells.
- Cytoplasm consists of all the material inside the cell except the nucleus and organelles.
- Mitochondria are organelles that produce energy from nutrients.
